coreSections.title: Data Governance
coreSections.description: Inventory, ownership, retention, and classification maturity.

recommendations.low: Build a data inventory, ownership map, retention schedule, and classification approach.
recommendations.mid: Formalize lifecycle controls and cross-border movement governance.

questions.title: Do you maintain an inventory of critical and personal data assets?
Can you distinguish personal, sensitive, employee, and regulated data categories?

coreSections.title: Infrastructure & Hosting
coreSections.description: Hosting placement, concentration risk, and resilience.

recommendations.low: Map single-provider dependency and define fallback paths.
recommendations.mid: Add documented mobility and failover assumptions.
recommendations.high: Keep resilience and placement governance current.

questions.title: Are jurisdictional constraints considered when placing workloads?
Do you understand backup, support, and observability data movement?

recommendations.low: Centralize admin identity and remove shared access patterns.
recommendations.mid: Strengthen lifecycle management and privilege review.

questions.title: Is MFA consistently enforced for sensitive systems and admin access?
Do you have auditable joiner-mover-leaver controls?
questions.help: Lifecycle discipline supports both security and evidence.

recommendations.low: Document cross-border flows and incident escalation paths.
recommendations.mid: Improve evidence for transfer decisions and reporting readiness.
recommendations.high: Keep transfer and incident governance tested.

questions.title: Do you know when data moves across borders and why?
questions.help: Transfer visibility is a core requirement.

questions.title: Do you maintain a documented incident response process with reporting triggers?

coreSections.title: Governance & Exit
coreSections.description: Decision governance, exit readiness, and review triggers.

recommendations.low: Assign ownership, define review triggers, and document exit scenarios.
recommendations.mid: Integrate legal, security, and procurement into technology decisions.
recommendations.high: Use periodic reviews and tabletop exercises.

questions.title: Are legal and regulatory considerations built into vendor and architecture decisions?
questions.help: Governance should happen before dependency hardens.

questions.title: Do you have fallback plans for critical vendors and platforms?
questions.help: Exit readiness is part of resilience.
